name: Dragonborn

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.desc: Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.desc: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type. Each creature in the area must make a saving throw (DC = 8 + CON mod + proficiency). A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save (half on success). Damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th, and 5d6 at 16th. Recharges on a short or long rest.

traits.name: Damage Resistance
traits.desc: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

age: Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10-year-old human child by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80.
alignment: Dragonborn tend to extremes, making a conscious choice for one side or the other in the cosmic war between good and evil.
sizeDescription: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds.

name: Dwarf

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.desc: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus.

traits.name: Dwarven Combat Training
traits.desc: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.desc: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.

subraces.name: Hill Dwarf
subraces.desc: As a hill dwarf, you have keen senses, deep intuition, and remarkable resilience.

traits.name: Dwarven Toughness
traits.desc: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

age: Dwarves mature at the same rate as humans, but they're considered young until they reach the age of 50. On average, they live about 350 years.
alignment: Most dwarves are lawful, believing firmly in the benefits of a well-ordered society. They tend toward good as well.
sizeDescription: Dwarves stand between 4 and 5 feet tall and average about 150 pounds.

name: Elf

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic cannot put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.desc: Elves do not need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply for 4 hours a day. After resting this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

subraces.name: High Elf
subraces.desc: As a high elf, you have a keen mind and a mastery of at least the basics of magic.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.desc: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: High Elf Cantrip
traits.desc: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.desc: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

age: Elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans. An elf typically claims adulthood around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.
alignment: Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly toward the gentler aspects of chaos.
sizeDescription: Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds.

name: Gnome

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Gnome Cunning
traits.desc: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

subraces.name: Rock Gnome
subraces.desc: As a rock gnome, you have a natural inventiveness and hardiness beyond that of other gnomes.

traits.name: Artificer's Lore
traits.desc: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus.

traits.name: Tinker
traits.desc: You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp). You can have up to three such devices active at a time.

age: Gnomes mature at the same rate humans do, and most are expected to settle into an adult life by around age 40. They can live 350 to almost 500 years.
alignment: Gnomes are most often good. Those who tend toward law are sages, engineers, researchers, scholars, investigators, or inventors.
sizeDescription: Gnomes are between 3 and 4 feet tall and average about 40 pounds.

name: Half-Elf

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic cannot put you to sleep.

traits.name: Skill Versatility
traits.desc: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

age: Half-elves mature at the same rate humans do and reach adulthood around the age of 20. They live much longer than humans, however, often exceeding 180 years.
alignment: Half-elves share the chaotic bent of their elven heritage. They value both personal freedom and creative expression.
sizeDescription: Half-elves are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall.

name: Half-Orc

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Savage Attacks
traits.desc: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

traits.name: Relentless Endurance
traits.desc: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

traits.name: Menacing
traits.desc: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

age: Half-orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.
alignment: Half-orcs inherit a tendency toward chaos from their orc parents and are not strongly inclined toward good.
sizeDescription: Half-orcs are somewhat larger and bulkier than humans, and they range from 5 to well over 6 feet tall.

name: Halfling

traits.name: Brave
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.desc: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.desc: When you roll a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

subraces.name: Lightfoot Halfling
subraces.desc: As a lightfoot halfling, you can easily hide from notice, even using other people as cover.

traits.name: Naturally Stealthy
traits.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

age: A halfling reaches adulthood at the age of 20 and generally lives into the middle of his or her second century.
alignment: Most halflings are lawful good. As a rule, they are good-hearted and kind, hate to see others in pain, and have no tolerance for oppression.
sizeDescription: Halflings average about 3 feet tall and weigh about 40 pounds.

name: Human
age: Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.
alignment: Humans tend toward no particular alignment. The best and the worst are found among them.
sizeDescription: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall.

name: Tiefling

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Hellish Resistance
traits.desc: You have resistance to fire damage.

traits.name: Infernal Legacy
traits.desc: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. At 3rd level, you can cast hellish rebuke as a 2nd-level spell once per long rest. At 5th level, you can cast darkness once per long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

age: Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.
alignment: Tieflings might not have an innate tendency toward evil, but many of them end up there. An independent nature inclines many tieflings toward a chaotic alignment.
sizeDescription: Tieflings are about the same size and build as humans.
